events.summary: sTec engaged Gibson Dunn as outside corporate counsel during the board's strategic alternatives review.
The sTec board launched a formal strategic alternatives review and authorized outreach to potential financial advisors about a possible sale of the company.
Company A, through its financial advisor, requested a preliminary meeting with sTec and circulated a draft NDA regarding a possible transaction.
Balch Hill urged the board to explore strategic alternatives in its December 6 activist letter.
The sTec board formed a special committee to review strategic alternatives, including a possible sale of the company.
Company B signaled interest by saying it intended to present sTec as a possible acquisition target to Company B management.
Company D contacted sTec to express interest in exploring a potential acquisition of the company.
The board decided to confidentially approach likely strategic buyers while also conducting a limited confirmatory outreach to financial buyers.
sTec entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch for financial advisory services.
At the special committee's direction, BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting prospective acquirers of sTec.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E.
notes: The agreement included a don't ask, don't waive provision.

events.summary: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D.
sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F.
notes: The agreement included a don't ask, don't waive provision.

events.summary: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G.
sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.
notes: This was an addendum to an existing NDA rather than a first-time NDA.

events.summary: Company D gave a verbal indication that it would pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share.
notes: The filing describes this as a verbal indication pending a written indication of interest.

events.summary: Company H contacted BofA Merrill Lynch to express interest in a potential transaction with sTec.
WDC submitted a written indication of interest at $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
Company G indicated that it would not continue in the process.
events.drop_reason_text: indicated it would not continue in the process.

events.summary: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.
notes: The agreement included a don't ask, don't waive provision.

events.summary: Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest at $5.75 per share in cash and sent a draft exclusivity agreement.
notes: The proposal was accompanied by a draft exclusivity agreement.

events.summary: Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest at $5.00 to $5.75 per share in cash.
BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting responses by May 28, 2013.
Company H remained interested in an acquisition but could not increase its indicated value range after being told the submitted range was insufficient to advance.
events.drop_reason_text:
Company H remained interested in a potential acquisition of the company but that
Company H was not able to increase its indicated value range.
notes: The special committee had earlier indicated that Company H's submitted range was not sufficient to move it forward.

events.summary: WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash with a marked-up merger agreement and ancillary drafts.
notes: The indication contemplated signing definitive documentation no later than June 3, 2013.

events.summary: The board requested best and final proposals from WDC and Company D by May 30, 2013.
WDC confirmed that $9.15 per share was its best possible price and that it remained focused on announcing a transaction on June 3, 2013.
notes: This was WDC's best-and-final price confirmation after the board's May 29 request.

events.summary: WDC said it was not prepared to move forward with a transaction and stopped its due diligence efforts.
events.drop_reason_text:
WDC was not prepared to move forward with a transaction
with sTec at that time. In addition, WDC discontinued their due diligence efforts with the company at that time.

events.summary: Company D said it was disengaging from the process because it could not actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks.
events.drop_reason_text:
Company D would not
be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.

events.summary: WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest at $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
notes: WDC said it was otherwise prepared to move forward on the transaction terms previously proposed on May 28, 2013.

events.summary: The board told WDC to submit a specific best offer supported by senior management instead of a range proposal.
WDC submitted a written indication of interest at $6.85 per share in cash as its best and final price.
notes: WDC said it would otherwise proceed on the transaction terms previously proposed on May 28, 2013.

events.summary: sTec and WDC entered into a clean team agreement to allow additional data-room access.
notes: This was a clean team agreement layered on top of the parties' existing confidentiality arrangements.

events.summary: sTec, WDC, and Merger Sub executed the merger agreement and related ancillary agreements on June 23, 2013.
notes: Merger Sub appears in the execution sentence but is represented through WDC rather than as a separate roster actor.

events.summary: WDC and sTec announced the execution of the merger agreement in a joint press release before trading opened on June 24, 2013.
notes: Classified as bid_press_release because the joint announcement concerned the signed transaction.

exclusions.explanation: The board's multi-year review of strategy and strategic alternatives is background context rather than a discrete dated sale-process event.
Pre-process third-party approaches, including WDC lunch conversations, were described as general and exploratory and the filing states that no specific transaction terms were proposed.
Company A's advisor circulated only a draft non-disclosure agreement; the filing does not say any NDA was executed.
The February 14, 2013 meeting discussed committee actions and advisor selection factors, but it does not record a new retention or other discrete taxonomy event.
Company C expressed interest only in acquiring particular or limited, select assets of sTec rather than the whole company.
Company D sent only a draft non-disclosure agreement in this block; the executed NDA is recorded later.
Company F said it was interested only in limited, select assets of sTec and had no further communications regarding a possible transaction.
Company E later indicated that it was interested only in limited, select assets of sTec, so the special committee ended discussions.
Beyond the named Companies C, E and F, the filing says six strategic acquirers wanted only limited, select assets, implying three additional unnamed partial-asset parties.
Populating the virtual data room is process support work, not a standalone event in the 20-type taxonomy.
BofA Merrill Lynch sent a non-disclosure agreement to Company H on May 2, 2013, but the filing does not state that the NDA was executed until May 8, 2013.
The May 8, 2013 quarterly-results announcement is not a sale-process or bid press release.
Management presentations, data-room access, and diligence calls are supporting process steps but not standalone events in the 20-type taxonomy.
The special committee's message that Company H's range was insufficient is treated as process feedback leading into the dated May 23 Company H drop rather than as a separate earlier drop event.

The board's decision to move forward with WDC and BofA Merrill Lynch's communication of that decision are target-side negotiation steps that do not create a separate proposal or execution event beyond the existing WDC proposal timeline.

The special committee recommendation, board authorization, and communication to WDC to move forward on the June 14 proposal are internal target-side selection and negotiation steps rather than separate taxonomy events.
Negotiations over ancillary agreements, merger-agreement markup, and don't-ask-don't-waive provisions are transaction-document drafting context rather than separate taxonomy events.
The June 19 through June 23 merger-agreement negotiation context overlaps the prior chunk and does not create a separate taxonomy event here.
The special committee and board approval-and-recommendation meetings are internal target-side deliberation steps; the discrete outcome event is the June 23 execution captured separately.
These blocks restate the June 23 board determination and recommendation already described in the surrounding approval narrative.
The reasons-for-merger headings, supporting factors, and risk bullets explain the board's rationale and merger terms but do not create new standalone taxonomy events.
The discussion of directors' and executive officers' merger-related interests is disclosure context rather than a process or outcome event in the extraction taxonomy.

The recommendation section restates the board's June 23 approval of the signed deal and solicits shareholder votes, but it does not add a new external action or separate board-decision event beyond the already-captured transaction outcome.
The statement that sTec has retained BofA Merrill Lynch is an undated restatement of the already-established advisor role rather than a new dated retention event in this chunk.
The fairness-opinion delivery summary, assumptions, valuation analyses, and comparable-company listings are explanatory proxy materials and do not create standalone taxonomy events.
The overlap heading naming Western Digital Corporation is carry-forward context from the prior chunk rather than a new process fact.

The selected-company, precedent-transaction, discounted-cash-flow, and premiums-paid analyses are fairness-opinion valuation materials; their reference ranges and comparable-transaction tables are not bidder proposals or standalone deal-process events.
The miscellaneous banker-disclosure section describes analytical caveats, engagement fees, and BofA Merrill Lynch conflict disclosures, none of which create a taxonomy event.
